report_name: Qilin Ransomware Blinds Defenses with Advanced EDR Killer, Abusing Vulnerable Drivers

threat_actors.name: Water Galura
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group deploying a sophisticated EDR killer to neutralize endpoint security solutions before encryption. The group uses DLL side-loading and a 'bring your own vulnerable driver' (BYOVD) technique to gain kernel-level access.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The Qilin EDR killer payload systematically unregisters or overwrites kernel callback routines for over 300 EDR products to blind them before encryption.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Side-Loading
attack_patterns.description: The attack is initiated by a legitimate application (e.g., FoxitPDFReader.exe) loading a malicious DLL (msimg32.dll) placed in the same directory.

attack_patterns.name: Dynamic-link Library Injection
attack_patterns.description: The malicious loader DLL (msimg32.dll) initiates a four-stage, in-memory loading process for the final EDR killer payload, avoiding writing malicious executables to disk.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The 'bring your own vulnerable driver' (BYOVD) technique is used, abusing a vulnerability in the legitimate rwdrv.sys (ThrottleStop.sys) driver to escalate privileges from user-mode to kernel-mode.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the attack involves the Qilin ransomware payload encrypting the victim's system after security defenses have been disabled.

malware.name: Qilin Ransomware
malware.description: The final payload deployed by the Water Galura group to encrypt victim systems after security defenses have been disabled.

malware.name: Qilin EDR Killer
malware.description: A payload that abuses a vulnerable driver (rwdrv.sys) to gain kernel memory access and unregister EDR monitoring callbacks, effectively disabling security products.

malware.name: msimg32.dll loader
malware.description: A malicious DLL loader, named msimg32.dll, used in a DLL side-loading attack to execute the EDR killer payload in memory. It forwards legitimate calls to the real DLL to maintain normal application behavior.

malware.name: hlpdrv.sys
malware.description: A second driver loaded by the malware to terminate unprotected EDR processes after their kernel callbacks have been disabled.

tools.name: ThrottleStop.sys
tools.description: A legitimately signed but vulnerable driver abused in a 'bring your own vulnerable driver' (BYOVD) attack. The malware renames it to rwdrv.sys and uses its vulnerability to gain arbitrary read/write access to kernel memory.

vulnerabilities.name: ThrottleStop.sys Arbitrary Kernel Memory Read/Write
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in the legitimately signed ThrottleStop.sys driver (renamed to rwdrv.sys by the malware) that allows a user-mode application to gain arbitrary read/write access to kernel memory, enabling privilege escalation.

indicators.name: Qilin Ransomware Associated File Names
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Qilin Ransomware Associated File Creation
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4789-a0b1-c2d3e4f5a6b7
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files associated with the Qilin ransomware's EDR killer module, including the loader DLL and vulnerable drivers.
references:
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/qilin-edr-killer-infection-chain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1068
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of ThrottleStop.sys, though its presence in unusual locations should be investigated.
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\msimg32.dll'
      - '\rwdrv.sys'
      - '\hlpdrv.sys'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files associated with the Qilin ransomware EDR killer module.

indicators.name: Qilin BYOVD Vulnerable Driver Service Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Qilin Ransomware Vulnerable Driver Service Creation
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4789-b0c1-d2e3f4a5b6c7
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new service registry key used by Qilin ransomware to load its vulnerable driver (rwdrv.sys) for kernel access.
references:
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/qilin-edr-killer-infection-chain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1068
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, as 'rwdrv' is a specific name used by the malware.
level: critical
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    EventType: 'CreateKey'
    TargetObject|endswith: '\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\rwdrv'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the service registry key used to load the vulnerable driver in the Qilin ransomware attack.

indicators.name: Suspicious Loading of Vulnerable rwdrv/ThrottleStop Driver
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Loading of Vulnerable rwdrv/ThrottleStop Driver
id: 2b3c4d5e-6f7a-4890-c1d2-e3f4a5b6c7d8
status: experimental
description: Detects the loading of the rwdrv.sys or ThrottleStop.sys driver, which is known to be vulnerable and is abused by Qilin ransomware in a BYOVD attack.
references:
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/qilin-edr-killer-infection-chain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1068
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of the ThrottleStop utility. Correlation with other suspicious activity is recommended.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: driver_load
detection:
  selection:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\rwdrv.sys'
      - '\ThrottleStop.sys'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the loading of the vulnerable driver used in the Qilin BYOVD attack.

indicators.name: EDR or Antivirus Service Stopped Unexpectedly
indicators.pattern:
title: EDR or Antivirus Service Stopped Unexpectedly
id: 3c4d5e6f-7a8b-4901-d2e3-f4a5b6c7d8e9
status: experimental
description: Detects when a common EDR or Antivirus service enters a stopped state. This can be an indicator of tampering by malware like the Qilin EDR killer.
references:
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/qilin-edr-killer-infection-chain/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative action, software updates, or system reboots. This rule is noisy and requires context.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: system
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 7036
    param1|contains:
      - 'Windows Defender'
      - 'MsMp'
      - 'Sense'
      - 'Carbon Black'
      - 'CrowdStrike'
      - 'Sentinel'
      - 'Cylance'
      - 'Sophos'
      - 'ESET'
      - 'McAfee'
      - 'Symantec'
      - 'Trend Micro'
    param2: 'stopped'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects when a common EDR or Antivirus service enters a stopped state, which could indicate tampering.

relationships.source_name: Qilin BYOVD Vulnerable Driver Service Creation
Qilin BYOVD Vulnerable Driver Service Creation
Suspicious Loading of Vulnerable rwdrv/ThrottleStop Driver
EDR or Antivirus Service Stopped Unexpectedly
